name: feat-ESE-0012-05
title: Particle system PoC: 10k GPU-driven particles
description:
Build a particle system proof-of-concept using the GPU compute infrastructure. 10,000+ particles spawned as ECS entities with Transform, Velocity, ParticleLife, and ParticleVisual components. A GPU kernel integrates position from velocity, applies gravity, ages particles, and fades alpha. Rendered via the existing Three.js view sync path.

Inspired by bevy_hanabi's GPU particle pipeline. For PoC, CPU handles spawning/despawning; GPU handles per-frame simulation.
acceptanceCriteria:
- ParticleTag, ParticleLife, ParticleVisual components defined
- GPU kernel: integrates position, applies gravity, updates age, fades alpha
- 10,000+ particles rendered at stable 60fps in browser
- Particles spawn from a point source, arc under gravity, fade and die
- Dead particles (age > maxAge) recycled by CPU system each frame
- Transform component is shared: GPU writes particle positions, Three.js view sync reads them
- FPS counter visible in demo
demoDeliverable: Browser demo: 10,000 particle fountain effect. Particles spawn upward from a point, arc under gravity, fade out as they age. FPS counter overlay shows stable 60fps. Toggle button switches between GPU kernel and equivalent CPU system to show performance difference. Console logs GPU dispatch time per frame.
testingScenarios:
- 10k particles maintain 60fps (measure via requestAnimationFrame timing)
- Particle positions match CPU reference within epsilon after N frames
- Dead particles are correctly recycled (entity count stays bounded)
- View sync correctly reads GPU-written Transform positions
- Gravity uniform affects arc trajectory
- Alpha fades to 0 as age approaches maxAge
stakeholderUnderstanding: The particle PoC is the first end-to-end validation of the GPU compute stack. It proves: WGSL generation works, buffer sync works, shared Transform readback works (Three.js renders GPU-written positions), and the performance win is real (10k particles at 60fps vs CPU bottleneck). The GPU/CPU toggle in the demo makes the performance difference visible and undeniable.
